Name: Prowler ThreatScore Compliance Framework for Lovable
Description: Prowler ThreatScore Compliance Framework for Lovable assesses the security posture of AI-built apps published from Lovable across four pillars: Identity and Access Management (auth, password policy, RLS), Attack Surface (workspace visibility, security headers, HTTPS), Secrets Management (no secrets in frontend, pre-publication review), and Application Hardening (Edge Function auth, CSP, storage privacy).

Requirements.Description: CAPTCHA / bot protection is enabled on Lovable auth forms

Attributes.Title: Bot protection on auth forms
Attributes.AttributeDescription: Signup, login, and password reset forms in Lovable apps must require CAPTCHA so that automated tools cannot spam accounts, enumerate users, or perform credential stuffing at scale.
Attributes.AdditionalInformation: Lovable's documented best practice is to enable CAPTCHA on every authentication-facing form. Combined with rate limiting, CAPTCHA dramatically reduces the success rate of automated attacks.

Requirements.Description: Lovable apps enforce a strong password policy

Attributes.Title: Strong password policy
Attributes.AttributeDescription: Lovable apps using password authentication must require at least 8 characters and include uppercase, lowercase, and numeric characters.
Attributes.AdditionalInformation: Weak password policies enable trivial credential-stuffing and brute-force attacks. The minimum baseline mirrors the Lovable Cyber best-practices guidance.

Attributes.Title: Auth rate limiting
Attributes.AttributeDescription: Signup, login, and password reset endpoints must enforce rate limits to slow down credential stuffing, account enumeration, and brute-force attacks.
Attributes.AdditionalInformation: Rate limits should be applied per-IP and per-user in the Edge Function backing the auth flow, returning HTTP 429 once exceeded.

Requirements.Description: Row Level Security is enabled on every Supabase table backing the app

Attributes.Title: RLS on all tables
Attributes.AttributeDescription: RLS is the final layer of defence behind frontend and backend logic. Every Supabase table containing user data must have RLS enabled and a matching policy that scopes rows to the authenticated user, team, or organization.
Attributes.AdditionalInformation: Tables without RLS are reachable through the public anon key; this is the single most common cause of catastrophic Lovable app data leaks.

Attributes.Title: Edge Function authentication
Attributes.AttributeDescription: Every Supabase Edge Function backing a Lovable app must validate the JWT and apply role/permission checks server-side. Critical behaviour must never rely on browser-only enforcement.
Attributes.AdditionalInformation: Lovable's guidance is explicit: 'critical behaviour never reaches the browser and every request is evaluated consistently.'

Requirements.Description: Internal Lovable apps use workspace visibility, not public

Attributes.Title: Workspace-only access for internal apps
Attributes.AttributeDescription: Apps tagged as internal must use Workspace visibility rather than Public so that only authenticated workspace members can reach them.
Attributes.AdditionalInformation: Public visibility for internal tooling exposes admin dashboards, staging environments, and weakly-protected functionality to the entire internet.

Requirements.Description: Published apps are served over HTTPS

Attributes.Title: HTTPS enforcement
Attributes.AttributeDescription: Every published Lovable app must be reachable only over HTTPS so that credentials, JWTs, and user data are encrypted in transit.
Attributes.AdditionalInformation: Pair HTTPS with HSTS (preload + includeSubDomains) to prevent downgrade attacks.

Requirements.Description: Required HTTP security headers are configured

Attributes.Title: Baseline security headers
Attributes.AttributeDescription: Published apps must respond with Content-Security-Policy, Strict-Transport-Security, X-Content-Type-Options, X-Frame-Options, and Referrer-Policy.
Attributes.AdditionalInformation: These headers mitigate XSS, clickjacking, MIME sniffing, downgrade, and referrer leak attacks.

Attributes.Title: Strict CSP
Attributes.AttributeDescription: The CSP returned by the published app must define default-src and must not contain wildcards or 'unsafe-inline'/'unsafe-eval' for script execution.
Attributes.AdditionalInformation: A permissive CSP is a permissive lie: it gives the appearance of XSS protection while still allowing attacker-injected scripts to execute.

Requirements.Description: Public storage buckets are not used for private user content

Attributes.Title: Storage bucket privacy
Attributes.AttributeDescription: Supabase storage buckets must be Private by default. Only buckets serving genuinely public assets should remain Public.
Attributes.AdditionalInformation: Public buckets allow any visitor with the URL to enumerate and download files. Combine Private buckets with storage RLS and signed URLs for downloads.

Requirements.Description: No secrets are bundled into the published frontend

Attributes.Title: No secrets in browser bundle
Attributes.AttributeDescription: Service-role keys, third-party API tokens, and private keys must never appear in the JavaScript bundle delivered to the browser.
Attributes.AdditionalInformation: A leaked Supabase service-role key bypasses RLS entirely. Anything shipped to the browser must be considered compromised the moment it ships.

Requirements.Description: Lovable's pre-publication security review has been completed

Attributes.Title: Pre-publication security review
Attributes.AttributeDescription: Lovable's built-in 'Review Security' workflow must be run, and every reported finding must be resolved, before an app is published.
Attributes.AdditionalInformation: The historical wave of Lovable app data leaks was driven by skipped pre-publication reviews; treating it as a release gate prevents the most common failure modes.
